refnamediv.refpurpose: the configuration for subordinate user ids

refsect1.title: DESCRIPTION
refsect1.para: Subuid authorizes a user id to map ranges of user ids from its namespace into child namespaces.

The delegation of the subordinate uids can be configured via the subid field in /etc/nsswitch.conf file. Multiple values can be specified, separated by whitespace. These values will be consulted in order. Setting this field to files configures the delegation of uids to /etc/subuid. Setting any other value treats the delegation as a plugin following with a name of the form libsubid_$value.so. If the plugin is missing or fails to load, it is ignored. If no plugin configures successfully, the subordinate uid delegation falls back to files.
Note that the search stops as soon as the user exists in a delegation database, even if no subids are defined for that user there.
Also note, that newusers, useradd, and usermod will only create entries in /etc/subuid if the subid delegation is not configured or files is the first configured value.

refsect1.title: LOCAL SUBORDINATE DELEGATION
refsect1.para:
Each line in /etc/subuid contains a user name and a range of subordinate user ids that user is allowed to use.
This is specified with three fields delimited by colons (:). These fields are:
This file specifies the user IDs that ordinary users can use, with the newuidmap command, to configure uid mapping in a user namespace.
Multiple ranges may be specified per user.

When large number of entries (10000-100000 or more) are defined in /etc/subuid, parsing performance penalty will become noticeable. In this case it is recommended to use UIDs instead of login names. Benchmarks have shown speed-ups up to 20x.

refsect1.title: SEE ALSO
refsect1.para:
login.defs5 ,
newgidmap1 ,
newuidmap1 ,
newusers8 ,
subgid5 ,
useradd8 ,
userdel8 ,
usermod8 ,
user_namespaces7 .
